title: Microsoft AI announces seven MAI models across reasoning, coding, image and voice
friendlyTitle: Microsoft launches its own family of AI models
summary: Microsoft AI announced seven in-house MAI models spanning reasoning, agentic coding, image generation, transcription and speech generation. The company also introduced Frontier Tuning, a model-adaptation approach intended for enterprise-controlled deployments.
friendlySummary: Microsoft announced new AI models for thinking, coding, images, speech and transcription. Some are aimed at developers and business users.
whyItMatters: Microsoft is signaling that it wants more control over the model layer rather than only packaging other labs’ models. If the models perform as claimed, developers may see more Microsoft-native options inside Copilot, Foundry, VS Code and third-party inference platforms.
friendlyWhyItMatters: More model choices can help people pick cheaper or faster AI tools for different jobs.
